title: Signals and Systems
summary: This page discusses classifications of signals including analog, digital, continuous-time, and discrete-time signals illustrated in Figure 1.11. It introduces periodic signals, defining them as signals that must extend from negative to positive infinity, and explains that a periodic signal can be generated by periodic extension of any segment of duration T0. Figure 1.13 demonstrates this concept with a periodic signal of period T0=6.

figures.caption: Examples of signals: (a) analog, continuous time; (b) digital, continuous time; (c) analog, discrete time; and (d) digital, discrete time.
